channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: China expands car exports as domestic market oversupply pushes growth abroad
item.description: Chinese carmakers are rapidly increasing their presence in international markets as overproduction at home drives them to export more vehicles. Despite China being the world's largest car market, a su...

item.title: Knicks Fans React to Trump's Attendance, Leading to NBA Watch Party Relocation
item.description: Fans of the New York Knicks have expressed their displeasure over President Donald Trump's planned attendance at Game 3 of the NBA Finals against the San Antonio Spurs. Once a New York resident, Trump...

item.title: South Africa adopts stricter illegal migration policy as Ramaphosa rejects xenophobia
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has announced a new and stricter approach to handling illegal migration in South Africa, combining stronger enforcement with a clear rejection of xenophobia and vigilantism. ...

item.title: Ariana Grande Opens Her 'Eternal Sunshine Tour' with a Stunning Oakland Performance
item.description: Ariana Grande kicked off her 'Eternal Sunshine Tour' at Oakland Arena with a 105-minute show that emphasized her latest album while maintaining a balanced mix of spectacle and musicality. Nearly half ...

item.title: Trump interview in Wisconsin interrupted multiple times by heavy rain
item.description: During a pre-recorded interview at Custer Farms in Wisconsin, President Donald Trump experienced multiple interruptions due to heavy rainfall. The interview, conducted by NBC News’ Kristen Welker and ...

item.title: Mary Trump Questions White House Explanation for President Trump's Repeated Cognitive Tests
item.description: A public disagreement has emerged over President Donald Trump's repeated cognitive testing after comments made by Dr. Mehmet Oz, a senior figure in the administration. During a White House briefing, O...

item.title: Senator Reports Severe Oversight Issues and Poor Conditions at Newark ICE Detention Center
item.description: During a recent visit to the Delaney Hall ICE detention facility in Newark, New Jersey, Democratic Senator Andy Kim (D-NJ) observed troubling conditions that raised serious concerns about the treatmen...

item.title: Comelec to investigate donors of Senators Escudero and Marcoleta over campaign contributions
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) has officially summoned several campaign donors linked to Senators Francis 'Chiz' Escudero and Rodante Marcoleta following complaints filed against them. Earlier,...

item.title: Study Shows Most Ghanaian Teachers Support Learner-Centred Learning but Still Depend on Traditional Classroom Authority
item.description: Many African countries are changing their school curricula to help students develop skills such as critical thinking, creativity, collaboration and problem-solving instead of relying mainly on memoris...

item.title: Gen Z Goliath Reveals His Baby Mama Left Him During Hard Times and Sought to Return After His Rise to Fame
item.description: Kenyan influencer Bradley Mtall, popularly known as Gen Z Goliath, has shared his side of the story about his past relationship. The tall guy who blew up on social media in 2024 because of his impress...

item.title: India Initiates Measures to Strengthen Financial Health of Airlines
item.description: The Indian aviation sector is facing significant financial stress, prompting the Union Aviation Ministry to start a comprehensive study on the structural challenges affecting airlines. The ministry ha...

item.title: Critics react to Trump's abrupt NBC interview exit amid false election claims
item.description: On June 7, 2026, President Donald Trump faced widespread criticism following a tense interview with NBC News journalist Kristen Welker. During the interview, Trump was questioned about his repeated fa...

item.title: China Begins Infrastructure Development for the CZ-9 Super Heavy Rocket with Potential Support for Extremely Large Payload Fairings
item.description: A newly published construction tender from Hainan Province provides the clearest evidence yet that China is moving forward with the development of the Long March CZ-9 super heavy-lift rocket. The proj...

item.title: Rare Early Photographs Trace Dean Martin’s Rise From Dino Crocetti to National Stardom
item.description: The article showcases a collection of portraits depicting Dean Martin during the late 1930s and 1940s, before he became one of the most recognizable entertainers in America. Born Dino Paul Crocetti in...

item.title: IEC Outlines Special Voting Rules Ahead of South Africa's Local Elections as Court Cases and Kenya Trade Talks Continue
item.description: This South African news briefing covers three major developments. First, the Electoral Commission of South Africa (IEC) has confirmed that special voting provisions will be available during the upcomi...

item.title: Late turnover by Wembanyama helps Knicks edge Spurs in Game 2 of NBA Finals
item.description: Victor Wembanyama acknowledged responsibility for a critical late-game mistake as the San Antonio Spurs fell 105-104 to the New York Knicks in Game 2 of the NBA Finals, leaving San Antonio in a 2-0 se...

item.title: Iranian Parliament Speaker Warns U.S. Bases Could Become Targets Following Escalation in Lebanon Conflict
item.description: According to the article, Iran issued a warning to the United States after a new wave of Israeli airstrikes struck Lebanon. The report states that tensions remain high as Washington and Tehran continu...

item.title: Brighton reject two Tottenham bids for centre-back Jan Paul van Hecke
item.description: Brighton & Hove Albion’s chief executive, Paul Barber, has confirmed that the club has turned down two separate transfer bids from Tottenham Hotspur for their Dutch centre-back Jan Paul van Hecke. The...

item.title: Opposition Questions President Ruto's Relationship with Zimbabwean Businessman Wicknell Chivayo
item.description: A political debate has emerged in Kenya over the close relationship between President William Ruto and Zimbabwean businessman Wicknell Chivayo. The controversy intensified after Chivayo visited the ne...

item.title: ISPR says security forces killed 27 militants during intelligence-based operations in North Waziristan
item.description: According to a statement issued by the Inter-Services Public Relations (ISPR), Pakistani security forces killed 27 militants during a series of intelligence-based operations conducted over the last 72...
